files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 90f266f..5771125 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -96,2 +96,9 @@ Day 91 / Week 13 observations:
 - Open issues are rising but still mostly same-day. Social criticism now centers on SLA enforcement, support handoffs, and usage limits rather than outages.
 - Week 13 adjustment: cautiously scale S2 content spend, add a little S2 support capacity, keep dev steady, and raise B/C quotas to improve professional value without making A too generous.
+
+Day 98 / Week 14 observations:
+- Cash jumped to $265k; subscription payments reached $250k against about $163k in weekly costs.
+- S2 content scaled well: 558 paid content subscribers from 2,877 leads, led by 376 plan C users. CPA and first-month revenue are both very favorable.
+- Cancellations dropped to 33 and S2 cancellations were low. Upgrades remain strong, and the active mix is shifting toward B/C.
+- Usage peaked around 1.1M/day, still safely within tier 3. Open issues are rising but remain near one-day resolution; social response to higher B/C quotas was positive.
+- Week 14 adjustment: scale S2 content more assertively, add matching S2 support capacity, keep prices/dev/tiers stable, and save toward tier-2 R&D once cash exceeds the $333k cost with a buffer.
